prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Extend ROCm quick reduce coverage
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
This PR extends the ROCm quick-reduce coverage. The kernel-level quick-reduce behavior now lives in `test_quick_all_reduce.py`, while the ROCm-specific file keeps the env parsing, communicator policy, and model-level end-to-end checks.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm] Add VLLM_ROCM_W8A8_TRITON_MAX_M env var for CK/Triton GEMM rou…
prs.author: rbrugaro-amd
prs.body_head:
…ting

Add a configurable batch-size threshold (VLLM_ROCM_W8A8_TRITON_MAX_M, default 16) to control when FP8 w8a8 blockscale GEMMs use Triton vs Composable Kernel (CK) on ROCm.

For small batch sizes (M <= threshold), Triton kernels are preferred as they perform better at low concurrency. For larger batches (M > threshold), CK kernels are used as they scale better at high concurrency.

Setting the variable to 0 forces CK for all batch sizes.

The dispatch uses `torch.cond` for compile-safe runtime branching,
following the same pattern as `FlashInferFp8DeepGEMMDynamicBlockScaledKernel`
on CUDA. The threshold is controlled by `VLLM_ROCM_W8A8_TRITON_MAX_M`
(default 16) and only activates for (N, K) shapes with a tuned Triton config.
Setting the variable to 0 forces CK for all batch sizes.
### Depends on
- #38958 — fixes `split_graph` `get_attr`/`placeholder` ordering for
  `torch.cond` compatibility with piecewise compilation. The fix from
  that PR is included here (cherry-picked) to make this branch testable
  standalone, but should be removed once #38958 is merged.
## Changes
- `vllm/model_executor/kernels/linear/scaled_mm/aiter.py` — Add
  `AiterFp8BlockScaledDynamicMMKernel` with `torch.cond` dispatch;
  register `dynamic_aiter_triton_ck_blockscale_gemm` custom op
- `vllm/model_executor/kernels/linear/__init__.py` — Add dynamic kernel
  as first priority in `_POSSIBLE_FP8_BLOCK_KERNELS[ROCM]`
- `vllm/compilation/backends.py` — Add `_move_get_attr_after_placeholders`
  (cherry-picked from #38958)
- `tests/compile/test_graph_partition.py` — Add `torch.cond` split_graph
  tests (cherry-picked from #38958)
- `tests/kernels/quantization/test_dynamic_aiter_triton_ck.py` — Add unit
  tests for dispatch routing, `can_implement` guards, and opcheck
## Verification (MI355x, DeepSeek-R1 FP8 TP8)
- **Compiled graph**: dynamic dispatch op on tuned shapes (qkv_a, q_b,
  o_proj, gate_up) with correct fallback to static CK on non-tuned
  shapes (d

prs.title: Fix LFM2 MoE decoding on ROCm
prs.author: tianshu-Michael-yu
prs.body_head:
## Summary

- Force `TRITON_ATTN` for `Lfm2MoeForCausalLM` on ROCm when the user has not explicitly selected an attention backend.
- Add focused config tests for ROCm auto-selection, explicit-backend preservation, and non-ROCm behavior.

## Why

On AMD GPUs, the default ROCm attention backend for `LiquidAI/LFM2-8B-A1B` can produce corrupted decode output after an initially plausible first token. Forcing `TRITON_ATTN` avoids the decode-time corruption and matches Hugging Face greedy generation on the validation prompts.

Baseline latest public ROCm wheel (`vllm==0.20.0+rocm721`) generated:
- `4..`
- `Paris, in the context of Manoto Di Tella Declaration for our discussion:`
- invalid JSON/gibberish continuation

Patched vLLM generated the same completions as Hugging Face:
- `4`
- `Paris`
- ```json
  {
    "color": "blue"
  }
  ```

## Validation

- `python -m py_compile vllm/model_executor/models/config.py tests/models/test_config.py`
- `git diff --check`
- `python -m pytest tests/models/test_config.py -q` (`3 passed`)
- Slurm on AMD MI325 (`gpuworker`, `tus1-p13-g26`), `torch==2.10.0+git8514f05`, HIP `7.2.53211`, `transformers==5.6.2`, patched `vllm==0.20.0+rocm721` site package:
  - HF reference job: `compare-lfm2-hf-rerun.json`
  - Public vLLM baseline job: `compare-lfm2-vllm-550279.out`
  - Patched vLLM job: `compare-lfm2-vllm-auto-fix-550430.out`

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Upgrade quantized FP4 kernels coverage
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
This PR upgrades current ROCm quantization kernel test coverage and upgrades it with new Triton, AITER, and MoE quantized unit tests. It expands the ROCm FP4 surface across AITER FP4 kernels, ROCm skinny gemms, compressed
tensors W4A16, ROCm-shaped Triton MoE kernels, and OCP-MX MoE TP targeting. It also adds an AITER MXFP4 MoE coverage on gfx950, so the ROCm AITER MoE file is no longer only a BF16 and FP8 test file. We group the MXFP4 MoE tests into single-GPU ROCm FP4 MoE, TP2, TP4, and TP8 OCP-MX derivatives.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Add missing quantization methods and fix online quant test failures
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Several quantization methods were failing on ROCm because they weren't registered in the platform's `supported_quantization` list, even though the underlying kernels already support them (either natively or through emulation fallbacks).

This adds `fp8_per_tensor`, `fp8_per_block`, `online`, `gpt_oss_mxfp4`, `mxfp8`, `modelopt`, `modelopt_mxfp8`, and `modelopt_mixed` to ROCm's supported quantization allowlist. These all have working codepaths on ROCm. FP8 variants use `ROCmFP8ScaledMMLinearKernel`, mxfp8 and modelopt_mxfp8 use `EmulationMxfp8LinearKernel` (introduced in #39205), and the online quant schemes resolve to fp8 methods that already work.

Also fixes two issues in the test infrastructure that were blocking the online quantization peak memory test on ROCm:

1. The peak GPU memory log in `base_loader.py` was gated on `is_cuda()` only, so it never fired on ROCm. Changed to `is_cuda_alike()` which covers both CUDA and ROCm since `torch.accelerator.max_memory_allocated()` works on both.

2. The memory thresholds in `test_online_quant_peak_mem` were hardcoded for CUDA's `fp8_e4m3fn`. ROCm on gfx950 uses `fp8_e4m3fnuz` which results in slightly higher memory usage (~7.45 GiB vs ~6.65 GiB), so the thresholds are now platform-aware.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [BugFix] Refactor add max_num_tokens_per_forward_pass to account for drafting
prs.author: LucasWilkinson
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Alternative bugfix to #34671. Solves a crash of specdec on main.

To solve the consistency issue, we add a `max_num_tokens_per_forward_pass` to the VllmConfig, that accounts for drafting.

## Testing

Tested with Qwen3-Next MTP and GSM8k repeated twice with various concurrencies. All pass with 85% accuracy, matching the non-spec baseline.

```
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct  \
  --tokenizer-mode auto  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8 \
  --speculative-config '{"method": "qwen3_next_mtp", "num_speculative_tokens": 5}' \
  --tensor-parallel-size 2 --port 8042
```
